issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: AskElephant
* Requester Name: Jake Allen
* Source: Internal
* Meeting/Pylon Link: 


**All right, so I have some product feedback on the meeting recording experience. We've had a customer and they often use the Zoom for their recording. And it's great, but sometimes because they have the same, sometimes their meetings have the same meeting URL, and sometimes those meetings go long, and sometimes the bot for the second meeting actually joins the first. **
****
****
**So the logical solution to this is, I mean, just use different meeting links. But then also, it still runs the chance of, okay, what if this meeting goes so long as to the bot in the waiting room for the second meeting actually times out? **
****
**One potential solution is you just increase the waiting time for the bot in the waitroom, but that's not ideal. And I don't know exactly the mechanisms in which it would make this happen, but ideally, there just should never be a scenario where a person has meetings on their calendar that don't get recorded. **
****
**We do have some thoughts as to how to make this happen. One potential thought is somehow we have the bot at the end of a meeting communicate back through a system that would then send a bot to next meeting. You know, it would signify, hey, my meeting has ended, you know, the next meeting can start now, whenever that is scheduled, and then it would perform a check to see when the calendar event actually starts, and if that time is in the past, then the bot automatically joins the meeting. **
****
**This is one potential solution, although I'm not an engineer per se. I don't know the exact limitations or specifications needed, but overall, the sentiment is,**

**I feel that if it's on your calendar, it should be recorded regardless. Because overall, you know, we want to capture meeting data, and if it is so hard to a person that they need to have to write into support like five different times to make sure that they get their meetings correctly recorded, that is so much time wasted and so much headache.**
****
**And yeah, to a certain extent, you know, you can argue that this is partially an onboarding issue, or maybe, you know, we haven't correctly, guided the user to understand how these mechanisms work. But I mean, if the mechanisms are this hard anyways to teach why not fix it? I mean, think about all the time that we would continue to spend teaching people how to do it this way. Wouldn't it be so much easier just to make it be easier to use?**

**And if somehow we could get to that point, I think that would save many people, including us internally, a lot of time and effort. So, thank you for listening.**
****
****
**
issues.title: an

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: Levver
* Requester Name: Nate Stoltenow
* Source: Pylon
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.usepylon.com/issues?conversationID=cab46142-a8a4-4136-8718-a1fd6cf3acfc](<https://app.usepylon.com/issues?conversationID=cab46142-a8a4-4136-8718-a1fd6cf3acfc>)

**Summary**

Customer needs to use variables in webhook URLs within the workflow builder to dynamically route webhook payloads to ClickUp. Currently blocked because the webhook node only accepts plain text URLs.

**Customer Quote(s)**

Customer is setting up a workflow that requires sending data to ClickUp via webhooks. Their use case requires dynamic webhook URLs (likely different endpoints based on workflow conditions or data), but the current webhook node implementation only accepts static plain text URLs with no variable interpolation support.

**Context / Details**

**Impact**

Blocks customer from automating their ClickUp integration workflows. Without variable support in webhook URLs, they cannot dynamically route data to different ClickUp endpoints based on workflow logic, forcing them to either create multiple separate workflows or handle routing outside the platform.

**Open Questions**
issues.title: Webhook Node does not allow variables for URL

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name:
* Requester Name:
* Source:
* Meeting/Pylon Link:

**Summary**

The Prompt Enhancer feature returns system-generated tokens/variables (e.g., "nmgt_2413214232113312322") instead of human-readable variable names, making prompts difficult to read and increasing the risk of user error. Users need a more intuitive way to identify what variables represent in their enhanced prompts.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "it returns variable/token as reference instead of returning a user-friendly and readable variable"
* Customer: "confuses users that suddenly have to read through X-digit long string in their prompt"
* Customer: "This is not a blocker, but makes potentially room for error"

**Context / Details**

* Affects the Prompt Enhancer feature in the Web App
* Current behavior: System displays machine-generated IDs like "nmgt_2413214232113312322"
* Desired behavior: Display readable variable names like "Variable_Engagement_ID" or "ID_Of_Selected_Engagement"
* Alternative solution suggested: Visual separation through color coding to distinguish variables from regular text

**Impact**

Creates confusion and friction in the user experience when reviewing enhanced prompts. While not blocking work, it increases cognitive load and potential for mistakes when users need to parse long alphanumeric strings to understand what variables represent in their prompts.
issues.title: Prompt Enahncer - do not use tokens/variables, but use "human language"

**Summary**

Pinny would like the RingCentral integration to auto-populate the meeting title on imports with both caller's caller ID information, so mainly just our name. Right now it populates with the phone numbers, but with so many calls and so many different people, it doesn't, you know, it doesn't make sense to memorize all the phone numbers, and so it is confusing, especially when looking through the search page.

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: Who First
* Requester Name: Mr. Joshua Oakes
* Source: Pylon
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.usepylon.com/issues/views/dbadd94c-9fc4-4f4b-8d78-90e131bc782a?conversationID=4c02891e-3510-4eb7-8353-b06927cbea91](<https://app.usepylon.com/issues/views/dbadd94c-9fc4-4f4b-8d78-90e131bc782a?conversationID=4c02891e-3510-4eb7-8353-b06927cbea91>)

**Summary**

The customer frequently uses the HubSpot agent and regularly experiences issues with the HubSpot agent where it will refuse to update the CRM unless a human approves the changes first. The customer would like the system to be fully automatic and has routinely included in his prompts instructions for the agent to move forward with updates without human verification.

**Customer Quote(s)**

"I’ve already added additional “do not wait for verification” instructions to that node. Piling more and more of those on like some kind of hocus pocus is crazy. This is on top of the HTML/payload failures."

**Context / Details**

Lots of different HubSpot Agents. Bro is low key really granular/picky but he probably makes a ticket about this every month and we can't just keep telling him to prompt it better. He said its reaching a point where he plans on not even using the agent anymore.

"The HubSpot agent keeps getting lippier no matter what I do. At this point I’m thinking of moving entirely to deterministic nodes."

Overall the customer would love some sort of toggle or way that the HubSpot agent can function autonomously.

**Impact**

In a world where we constantly feed customers the idea that we can update their CRM and they don't have to do admin work, we sure are creating a ton of admin work.

**Open Questions**

From Customer:  
"Was there a change to the system prompt or something?"

"Can we get an explicit “do not wait for validation” toggle on the HubSpot agent nodes to override this?"
issues.title: [Bug]: HubSpot Agent Keeps Asking for Human in the Loop.

issues.description:

* Company Name: GET PEYD

**Summary**

Pinny wants to be able to determine whether or not a meeting will record based solely on its title. He has a meeting that is scheduled at a different time every week, but it's more or less the same meeting. It's always titled the same thing, and he never wants it to record, but because it's not a recurring meeting, it's at a different time every week, he has to manually toggle off the recording every time.

**Customer Quote(s)**

"Thats the thing is its not a recurring meeting, its scheduled at a different time every week."

**Context / Details**

**Impact**

This would supply additional recording options that are currently unavailable, giving people the ability to adjust for minute and edge cases.

**Open Questions**

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: CHILI Publish
* Requester Name: Erik De
* Source: Meeting
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KFXMMHDBJBSQ0CJT03CBE60P](<https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KFXMMHDBJBSQ0CJT03CBE60P>)

**Summary**

Erik needs the ability to save filtered search views on the SEARCH page so he can quickly access his team's calls without manually reselecting team members each time. Currently, he must manually apply filters every time he wants to review team call data, creating friction in his workflow for team-level analysis and insights.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "What I had previously on the previous tool, right, is a view of all calls from my team. Is there any, like, team structure that we can set up like that?"
* Customer: "Is there a way to save those filters?"
* Customer: "So whenever I want to have a view of what calls were done by the team, I need to go and select the team members."

**Context / Details**

* Erik is the Director of Sales and manages a team that includes account executives like Seb Charlton
* His previous tool provided a persistent team-level view of all calls
* AskElephant currently offers individual call filtering via the SEARCH page with filter options (by team member, participant type, etc.)
* Chili Publish has groups functionality available, but saved filter views are not currently supported
* Erik's workflow requires frequent review of team call data for coaching, performance management, and RevOps optimization
* Timezone: Belgium (Europe/Brussels)

**Impact**

* **What breaks today:** Erik cannot quickly access a standardized view of his team's calls. He must manually select multiple team members through filters each time, creating repetitive manual work and friction in accessing team-level insights.
* **Why it matters:** As Director of Sales managing RevOps optimization, Erik needs efficient access to team call data for:
  * Team performance analysis and coaching
  * Identifying trends across multiple conversations
  * Supporting revenue operations workflow optimization
  * Comparing team member call patterns and outcomes
* **What this would enable:** Saved filter views would allow Erik to instantly access pre-configured team call sets with one click, enabling faster data-driven decision-making and reducing operational friction.

**Open Questions**

* Should saved filters be at the individual user level, or should managers be able to share saved filters across their team?
* Are there specific filter combinations Erik most frequently uses that could inform the MVP?
* Should saved filters include date ranges, or just team member selections?
issues.title: Saved filter on search page

**Customer Quote(s)**

Germany is one of the strictest GDPR enforcement environment in the EU, and the GDPR rules already apply to you today.  So the fact that you made the app available only for “certain countries”  in Europe because you were afraid that certain individuals would think you were GDPR compliant in accordance to the european regulations feels rather curious.*
* **Veronika**: *Also, it is unclear to me - again, not a lawyer here - how can AskE claim the GDPR compliance while the app itself is not GDPR compliant? It was made very clear to us during the sales process that AskElephant is fully GDPR compliant — does this not apply on the phone app as well?*

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: CHILI Publish
* Requester Name: Veronika
* Source: Meeting
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J](<https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J>)

**Summary**
The current automation visibility model restricts users from seeing automations that apply to them, causing information gaps and duplicate requests. Veronika expects a role-based hierarchy where Owners see all automations, Managers see automations for their organizational units, and Users see their own automations—enabling self-service discovery and reducing RevOps overhead.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* **Veronika:** *"So I foresee that as an issue where one of the 21 people in our organization will be like, oh, I I want this workflow. And then I'm like, it's already there. And they're like, oh, but I don't know what it does. And I tell them it does the and and we end up in a one hour long call simply because they don't see what is already running for them."*1
* **Veronika:** *"So the name of the automation is what will then also appear in the account conversation wherever I let that automation sit, if it's a manually triggered one. The name will then be appearing in that long bubble. Right? So if I go with potentially long name for the automation Uh-huh. I would have a super long bubble there. Right?"*1
* **Veronika:** *"It would be great if there was an option to, like, build a user friendly label. Because if I if I know my automation will be used by salespeople, I can name it accordingly. But for my internal data keeping of the automations, I would follow the the notation of team manual automated dash whatever name. Right?"*1
* **Veronika:** *"That that was surprising to me because, you know and I'm not saying this is the correct way. It's it's just something that I would have expected for one reason or another is to owner of the platform. So person with the role of the owner can see everything Manager sees only automations in the organizational unit where they are a manager. So Eric is a manager. He should see his and his team's automations, and then users should see their automations."*1

**Context / Details**

* **Current Visibility Model:**
  * Only Owners and creators see automations
  * Regular users cannot see automations that apply to them
  * Leads to duplicate automation requests and confusion about what's already running
* **Expected Role-Based Hierarchy:**
  * **Owner:** Sees all automations workspace-wide
  * **Manager:** Sees automations for their organizational unit + team automations
  * **User:** Sees automations they created + automations assigned/applicable to their role/team
* **Related Challenge: Automation Naming & Labels:**
  * Manual trigger automation names appear in UI as "bubbles" (action buttons in conversation view)
  * Long automation names create cluttered, hard-to-read buttons
  * Veronika wants **separate internal naming** (e.g., "sales-deal-stage-update") and **user-facing labels** (e.g., "Update Deal")
  * This mirrors Salesforce's field label vs. API name pattern
* **Naming Convention Best Practice (Current):**
  * Standard prefix: `team-automation-name` (e.g., `sales-quarterly-forecast-update`)
  * Allows filtering by team in automation list
  * But does NOT solve the user-facing label problem
* **Current Workaround:**
  * RevOps maintains external Notion doc of all automations (manual, time-consuming, often outdated)
  * Users must ask RevOps which automations exist rather than self-discovering

**Impact**

* **Users cannot see automations that apply to them**
  * Creates duplicate automation requests as users think the workflow doesn't exist
  * Leads to 1+ hour troubleshooting calls to clarify what's already running
  * Reduces user confidence in the platform ("I didn't know this existed")
* **RevOps spends excessive time answering visibility questions**
  * Time spent on "is this automation already running?" instead of building new workflows
  * Becomes a bottleneck for automation adoption and feature expansion
  * Reduces overall ROI on the AskElephant platform
* **No clear visibility into automation lifecycle**
  * Difficult to identify which automations are actively used vs. inactive/orphaned
  * Leads to cluttered automation lists that are hard to navigate
  * Creates "automation debt" (unused workflows consuming workspace resources)
* **Long automation names create poor user experience**
  * Manual trigger buttons display full automation names as "bubbles" in conversation view
  * Long names create cluttered, unreadable buttons that confuse end users
  * Users cannot distinguish between similar automations at a glance
* **RevOps must maintain external documentation to compensate**
  * External Notion doc required to map automations to teams/use cases
  * Documentation is manual, time-consuming, and frequently outdated
  * Perpetuates the visibility problem (users still don't know what exists in AskElephant)
* **Self-service discovery enables adoption**
  * Users can see automations applicable to their role/team without RevOps intervention
  * Reduces support burden and empowers end users to explore platform capabilities
  * Increases automation adoption rate across the organization
* **Cleaner, more intuitive UI**
  * User-friendly labels for manual triggers while maintaining internal naming conventions (e.g., label "Update Deal" internally named "sales-deal-stage-update")
  * Mirrors Salesforce's field label vs. API name pattern—familiar to most GTM teams
  * Reduces cognitive load when using manual automations
* **Organizational clarity and accountability**
  * Managers see what's running in their unit; owners maintain workspace overview
  * Clear ownership model (who created/owns each automation)
  * Enables better change management and automation governance
* **Better automation lifecycle management**
  * Easier to identify and clean up unused/orphaned automations
  * Enables automated flagging of inactive workflows for review/deletion
  * Reduces technical debt over time

**Open Questions**

* Should "Users" see only their own automations or also team automations based on group membership?
* For manual-trigger automations, should users see ALL available automations or only those relevant to their context (e.g., sales rep sees sales automations)?
* Should there be an automation visibility log (who modified, when, why) for audit/governance purposes?
* Should inactive/orphaned automations be automatically archived or flagged for deletion?
* How should the user-facing label feature interact with multi-language support (if planned)?
* Should managers have the ability to delegate automation creation/management to team leads?
issues.title: Users/Teams cannot filter workflows down to ones that only apply to them

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: CHILI Publish
* Requester Name: Veronika
* Source: Meeting
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J](<https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J>)

**Summary**

CHILI publish standardizes on Microsoft 365 (Teams, SharePoint, Office 365) as its collaboration platform, but AskElephant lacks integrations for Teams messaging, SharePoint document access, and PowerPoint generation. Veronika needs the ability to post call summaries to Teams, access/update SharePoint documents, and auto-generate presentation decks from call insights—enabling faster stakeholder communication and reducing manual presentation creation.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* **Veronika:** *"I'm using Microsoft Teams, and I I apologize for that because it was some reason we keep using Microsoft Teams. I guess, because it's free. And I I hate every single day of my work life."*1
* **Veronika:** *"Microsoft Teams. Potentially, also other Microsoft tools, so something like SharePoint access or or whatever Microsoft three sixty five so that we could generate PowerPoint presentations, for instance. This is a lower priority, but it's part of our tool stack."*1

**Context / Details**

* **Microsoft Teams Use Case:**
  * Post call summaries, action items, and key insights directly to Teams channels
  * Enable real-time team alignment without leaving AskElephant
  * Route notifications to relevant channels (e.g., #sales-updates, #customer-success)
* **SharePoint Use Case:**
  * Access and update collaborative documents stored in SharePoint
  * Push call documentation to shared folders for team access
  * Enable bidirectional sync between AskElephant insights and SharePoint records
* **PowerPoint Use Case:**
  * Auto-generate presentation decks from call data and summaries
  * Populate slides with customer context, insights, action items, and timelines
  * Reduce manual presentation creation for stakeholder updates or client deliverables
* **Priority:** Medium-High (Teams, SharePoint) → Lower (PowerPoint generation initially, but valuable long-term)
* **Roadmap Context:** AskElephant is integrating **Composio**, which includes Microsoft 365 connectors. Expected launch within weeks.

**Impact**

* **What Breaks Today:**
  * Call insights require manual copy-paste into Teams for team visibility
  * PowerPoint presentations must be created manually from call summaries—labor-intensive and error-prone
  * SharePoint documents are not connected to AskElephant insights; no unified collaboration experience
  * Team alignment on call outcomes is delayed because context is trapped in AskElephant
* **What This Enables:**
  * **Automations** post formatted summaries to Teams channels immediately after calls
  * **PowerPoint decks** auto-generate with customer context, action items, and timelines
  * **Team collaboration** centered around shared documents with call insights embedded
  * **Faster stakeholder communication** without manual presentation work
  * **Process standardization** across all Microsoft 365-integrated workflows
* **Why It Matters:**
  * CHILI publish has already standardized on Microsoft 365 (even if reluctantly, as Veronika notes). Connecting AskElephant to Teams, SharePoint, and PowerPoint makes the platform indispensable for daily GTM workflows. Currently, insights stay isolated; integrations would make AskElephant the hub for conversation → action → communication.**Open Questions**

**Open Questions**

* Should Teams messages be posted to specific channels based on call type or team (Sales, CS, Implementation)?
* What information should PowerPoint decks include? (e.g., call summary, customer goals, pain points, action items, next steps, timeline)
* Should SharePoint integration support only document creation or also updates to existing documents?
* Is Composio the planned delivery mechanism, or should direct Microsoft Graph API integration be prioritized?
* Should PowerPoint generation be included in Phase 1, or deferred as a Phase 2 capability?
issues.title: Microsoft 365 Integration

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: CHILI Publish
* Requester Name: Veronika
* Source: Meeting
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J](<https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J>)

**Summary**

CHILI publish uses Atlassian tools (Jira, Confluence) as core systems for issue tracking and knowledge management, but lacks native AskElephant integrations. Veronika needs the ability to create and update Confluence pages and Jira issues directly from call insights, enabling automated handoff documentation and closing the gap between conversation insights and tracked action items.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* **Veronika:** *"So very simple use case would be something like allowing me to create a Confluence page as a result of a call, but also create an update Jira issues. So it's it both Jira and and Confluence."*1

**Context / Details**

* **Confluence Use Case:**
  * Create knowledge base pages automatically after customer/internal calls
  * Populate pages with call summaries, customer goals, pain points, and implementation considerations
  * Enable team visibility into post-call documentation without manual transcription
* **Jira Use Case:**
  * Create or update issue tickets based on call outcomes and action items
  * Link call insights to existing tickets (e.g., feature requests, bugs, implementation tasks)
  * Automatically populate ticket fields: description, assignee, labels, due dates from call context
* **Workflow Context:**
  * Sales calls → Auto-create Confluence "Post-Call Summary" page + Jira tickets for CS/Implementation
  * Eliminates manual data entry and reduces handoff friction between teams
  * Ensures customer requirements are tracked and visible across the organization
* **Roadmap Context:** AskElephant is integrating **Composio**, an agentic middleware platform that includes Atlassian connectors. Expected launch within weeks.

**Impact**

* **What Breaks Today:**
  * Call insights cannot be automatically pushed to Jira or Confluence
  * Post-call documentation requires manual transcription by RevOps or team members
  * Customer requirements captured in calls are not automatically tracked in Jira
  * CS/Implementation teams miss context because insights stay siloed in AskElephant

  **What This Enables:**
  * **Automations** create Confluence pages with standardized post-call summaries
  * **Action items** automatically become Jira tickets with full context (customer goals, timelines, owners)
  * **Handoff consistency** ensures no information loss between Sales → CS → Implementation
  * **Single source of truth** for customer requirements across tools
  * **Reduced manual work** for RevOps and team leads
* **Why It Matters:**
  * AskElephant's core value is turning conversations into outcomes. Without Atlassian integrations, customer intelligence remains trapped in transcripts. Connecting to Jira + Confluence enables the full GTM workflow: capture → analyze → act → track → execute.

**Open Questions**

* Should Confluence page templates be customizable per team (Sales vs. CS vs. Implementation)?
* What Jira fields should auto-populate from AskElephant insights (assignee, sprint, labels, etc.)?
* Should the integration support bi-directional sync (AskElephant → Jira → AskElephant)?
* Is Composio the planned delivery mechanism, or should direct Atlassian API integration be prioritized?
issues.title: Atlassian Integration (Jira & Confluence)

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: Chili Publish
* Requester Name: Veronika
* Source: Meeting
* Meeting/Pylon Link: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J](<https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KG2RTSW4TCHX0FGSTXDGR68J>) 

**Summary**

Users lack granular control over call deletion and privacy settings, forcing RevOps to grant elevated permissions (Manager role) as a workaround—which inadvertently grants visibility to ALL automations, creating an unintended security and privacy gap. This breaks the principle of least privilege and exposes sensitive automation logic to users who should only access their own workflows.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* **Veronika:** *"So we had the issue of one of our user wanted to delete their call because it was an internal meeting. He did not want it to be in AskElephant at all. So but it turned out a user cannot delete even though it's their own call."*
* **Veronika:** *"I had to bump them to a manager so that they can delete. And now as a manager, they can see automation. So it kinda you know, it's a workaround for a decision done around deletions and permissions on that level."*
* **Veronika:** *"Because now the same user will be suddenly able to see all automations. Right? Because managers can see the automations."*
* **Veronika:** *"Why why can't user mark a meeting as private?"*

**Context / Details**

* **Scenario 1 (Deletion):** A user created an internal meeting they wanted removed from AskElephant entirely. Since the system restricts deletion to Manager+ roles, the only workaround was to elevate the user to Manager—granting them unintended visibility into ALL team automations.
* **Scenario 2 (Privacy):** Users cannot mark their own calls as private. Only Managers and Owners have this capability. Internal calls (budget discussions, staff reductions, sensitive topics) require privacy controls but lack user-level granularity.
* **Current Experience:** Call privacy/deletion is binary (Managers/Owners control it). Calls default to public unless a Manager/Owner explicitly sets them private.
* **Upcoming Experience:** A Privacy Agent rule engine (launching within weeks) will allow workspace-level privacy rules (e.g., "if keywords=investors, mark private") with exceptions and simulations.

**Impact**

* **What Breaks Today:**
  * Least-privilege principle is violated—granting deletion/privacy rights forces exposure to all automations
  * RevOps cannot safely escalate user permissions without collateral access
  * Internal sensitive calls (payroll, layoffs, budgets) lack user-level privacy control
  * Users must contact RevOps for every internal call deletion or privacy adjustment, creating friction
* **What This Enables:**
  * Users gain self-service control over internal calls without security gaps
  * RevOps maintains oversight of external/customer data without granting automation visibility
  * Internal meeting privacy becomes contextual (rules-based) rather than manual
  * Reduced support tickets and faster user workflows
* **Why It Matters:**
  * Data governance, user autonomy, and organizational trust depend on respecting call sensitivity and limiting exposure to internal process logic (automations). Currently, AskElephant forces a false choice: either restrict users entirely or expose them to everything.

**Open Questions**

* Should user-level deletion be restricted to calls they personally created/attended, or all personal calls?
* Should the Privacy Agent launch before or after this granular permission model is finalized?
* Are there compliance/audit considerations for internal call deletion that require Manager approval trails?
issues.title: Users have control over their own meeting deletion/privacy

Need confirmation on page context and full repro.

issues.description:
## Problem

Customers believe call downloads are not supported because AskElephant tells them it can't, even when capability exists.

## Evidence

* Slack thread: [https://askelephant.slack.com/archives/C093A29CKPU/p1769716679858399?thread_ts=1769716679.858399](<https://askelephant.slack.com/archives/C093A29CKPU/p1769716679858399?thread_ts=1769716679.858399>)
* Quote: "Jillian @ Design Ergonomics thought she couldn't download calls because AskElephant said it can't."

## Expected

If downloads are supported, UI and system responses should reflect capability. If not supported for some roles/workspaces, messaging should be explicit with guidance.

## Impact

Confusion erodes trust and creates avoidable support load.

## Notes

Coordinate with CS to clarify current capability and update copy/UX accordingly.

**Customer / Account**

* Company Name: Intelligent Technical Solutions
* Requester Name: Stephen Jenkins
* Source: Pylon

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Unknown
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD4VNHCW13KB29NMTW0BTVWR](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD4VNHCW13KB29NMTW0BTVWR) (2025-12-23)

**Summary**
Customer wants a per-customer “home dashboard” that tracks progress against key outcome metrics (e.g., deal velocity, retention, time saved) by role/team and provides very clear next steps so reps can "cut through" complexity.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "you go to a customer and you basically see, like, a home dashboard for that customer"
* Customer: "Progress for each of those and where you're at and very clear next steps."
* Customer: "help me, AskElephant. Just cut through like so much of the complexity and bullshit. Just help me know what I freaking need to do."

**Context / Details**

* They discussed role-based outcome buckets (examples mentioned):
  * AEs: deal velocity, win/close rate, time saved, ICP alignment, likely-to-close %
  * CSMs: churn/"term" prevention, expansion, time saved, retention
  * Enablement: operational scaling, process consistency, reporting accuracy
  * Solutions/Implementation: customer's time to value, handoff quality, ramp efficiency
  * RevOps: forecasting accuracy, pipeline health, revenue trends
* Desired output seems to combine: where customer is + progress vs outcomes + next actions.

**Impact**
Without an outcomes + next-steps dashboard, reps must mentally track goals/progress and manually determine what to do next, limiting scale and consistency.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Which outcome metrics should be configurable vs default?
* Where should the source-of-truth for metric values live (AskElephant vs CRM vs manual input)?
issues.title: Customer dashboard should show progress vs role-based outcome metrics + clear next steps

issues.description:

* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD4VPWHT9QW36C2VPD1ZQJAK](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD4VPWHT9QW36C2VPD1ZQJAK) (AI-Powered Sales Dashboards for Reps, 2025-12-22)

**Summary**
Customer wants dashboards to be AI-first and proactive (“talking to me”), not a passive place humans go to review data.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: “the whole point of dashboards is so that a human can review what needs action… And that doesn't feel like that's AI first.”
* Customer: “I have to go to post hoc with a question. It's never, like, talking to me.”
* Customer: “No. Generative UI dashboards. But they're still triggered by humans. So how do you have them be proactive?”

**Context / Details**

* Feedback contrasts traditional CRM dashboards (“so much data that the reps don't use it”) with a more proactive, AI-driven experience.
* Customer used metaphor: dashboard hub should “feel alive” (like an automated warehouse), implying continuous monitoring + surfaced insights without manual querying.

**Impact**
Dashboards that require manual interrogation create “brain rot” and put too much work on users; reps/leaders may not use them.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What exact proactive surfaces are desired (in-product feed, notifications, auto-generated views), and what cadence/controls are needed?

Project: UX/UI General
Milestone: Navigation
Priority: Medium
issues.title: AI-first proactive “living dashboard” vs passive dashboards

**Summary**
Customer wants a single, unified “customer journey” view (not split into pre-sales vs post-sales) that shows exactly where each client is, even when the journey loops due to resells, multi-threading, and multiple deals.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "We wanna think of a customer journey as just one single journey."
* Customer: "sometimes you loop back as you resell products. So there's the whole aspect of also multi threading, and then multi deals, and then going back."
* Customer: "You guys will know exactly where your clients are... In the journey."

**Context / Details**

* Current framing in market/tools: separate pre-sales journey vs sales process vs post-sales process.
* Desired: one continuous journey model that can be dynamic and loop.
* Intended users: sales + CS needing shared visibility and context continuity.

**Impact**
Without a unified journey view, teams lack a consistent way to understand and communicate where a customer is across the full lifecycle.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What are the specific journey stages/milestones they expect in the unified model?
* Should the system support multiple concurrent journeys/deals per company/contact?
issues.title: Customer dashboard: single unified customer journey across sales + CS (with looping/multi-deal)

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: AskElephant
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD48D18RQTB8RVRRSE5V6GJD](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD48D18RQTB8RVRRSE5V6GJD) (2025-12-22 MST)

**Summary**

* Users may naturally create a second rule for an exception later, which can create conflicting privacy rules; the UI should better guide/condition users to add exceptions to an existing rule.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Your instinct is gonna be, like, create new rule to talk about this one on one with Brian. Right?"
* Customer: "You're not gonna know to go back to that rule that you made on Thursday to add the exception there."

**Context / Details**

* Example discussed: one rule "mark all my one on ones private" and another separate rule "don't mark my one on one with Brian private".
* Team discussed showing exceptions inline ("and unless") and potentially adding better messaging to indicate "this is an exception".

**Impact**

* Conflicting rules can confuse the system and the user, leading to incorrect privacy outcomes.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What UI affordance should help users discover and edit an existing rule to add exceptions?

---

Classification: type/improvement
Product Area: area/security-&-compliance
Project + Milestone (triage suggestion): Security & Compliance → Data Privacy
Priority (triage suggestion): Medium
issues.title: Rule builder: conflicting rules risk—guide users to add exceptions vs creating separate rules

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: AskElephant
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD48D18RQTB8RVRRSE5V6GJD](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD48D18RQTB8RVRRSE5V6GJD) (2025-12-22 MST)

**Summary**

* In the privacy testing UI, users want to be able to manually apply the privacy outcome (e.g., mark a meeting private) right from the test screen, rather than having to navigate to the meeting page.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "If I want this private, well, how do I mark it private now?"
* Customer: "Why the fuck would I go to a meeting page? Because it's right here."
* Customer: "You like it? Sweet. Apply."

**Context / Details**

* The proposed test flow currently emphasizes: "This test doesn't impact any real calls" (mock test).
* Discussion suggests adding an explicit manual action (e.g., toggle/radio + "Apply") so users can change privacy on the meeting(s) from the test results.
* There’s concern users are conditioned from workflow testing that tests can run “real stuff,” so the UI needs clear messaging/confirmation about what is simulated vs applied.

**Impact**

* Without an in-place apply action, users may feel blocked/frustrated and lose trust in the privacy workflow; also increases navigation friction.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What should the confirmation/permission step be for applying to one meeting vs multiple vs all past meetings?

---

Classification: type/improvement
Product Area: area/security-&-compliance
Project + Milestone (triage suggestion): Security & Compliance → Data Privacy
Priority (triage suggestion): Medium
issues.title: Privacy rule testing: allow marking meetings private/public directly from test results ("Apply")

**Summary**
Customer is asking for a free or viewer seat so internal stakeholders (CEO, CS, marketing) can access and review sales calls without needing a paid seat or recording their own calls.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "So there's not like a free free seat, a viewer seat? Like Grain has paid seats... But then there's these free seats... you can go in and review a call."
* Customer: "CS... I wanna go see what that sales rep said... But they don't have access. They're not recording any of their own stuff."

**Context / Details**

* Customer uses Grain today; Grain allows unlimited free viewer seats for people in the org.
* They want broader internal access for call review and handoffs without needing every stakeholder to be a paid user.

**Impact**
May slow adoption across CS/marketing/leadership; creates friction/cost concerns for wider rollout and replacement of Grain.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What permissions should a viewer seat have (view only, comment, clip, export)?
* Should viewer seats be limited by role/domain, usage, or number?

**Summary**
Customer wants a forecast/manager view similar to Gong's deal board: view open/won/lost deals with an activity-by-date map over the last \~90 days to assess engagement and likelihood to close during forecast meetings.

**Context / Details**

* Mentioned for both mobile app and desktop.

**Impact**
Risk of complete data loss for recorded conversations reduces trust and limits use for on-the-go/in-person recording.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Unknown: Does the data loss occur on iOS, Android, and/or desktop specifically?

Request to add a human-in-the-loop experience for CRM updates that shows exactly what would change, allows the user to edit fields inline, and optionally requires approval before pushing updates.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "there'll be a human in the loop component. So it does CRM update. You're gonna have an actual visual readout that shows, like, this bill was changed to this."
* Customer: "you could make edits right there and be like, oh, actually, I wanted this field to change to this instead."
* Customer: "option of requiring human in the loop where we'll actually push the update until you approve it"

**Context / Details**

* Discussed in context of CRM agent improvements.
* Desired UI: interactive readout of changed fields; supports edits; approval gating.

**Impact**
Without review/approval, customers have less trust/visibility into CRM updates and can’t easily correct field-level changes before they’re applied.

\*\*Open Questions (if needed)\*\*n- Unknown: Which CRMs must be supported first (HubSpot vs others)?

* Unknown: Should approval be required by default or configurable per workflow/user?
issues.title: CRM agent: add human-in-the-loop review UI (show proposed field changes + allow edits + approval gate)

**Summary**

Customer feedback that the pitch should start with benefits/results rather than listing product features, because they get many similar AI calls and need the outcome fast.

**Context / Details**

* Cold call to sales director.
* Customer said they receive "a 100 of these calls" and hear the "new latest and greatest AI thing" often.
* Customer reacted positively to leading with a quantified pain-point summary from analyzed calls, and asked to focus on outcomes.

**Impact**

Current positioning may cause prospects to disengage early because messaging sounds like other AI tools and doesn't quickly communicate outcomes.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Unknown: Should this be addressed via sales enablement materials, in-product messaging, or both?
* Unknown: Where should the "benefits/results" messaging be surfaced (website, outbound scripts, in-app, demo flow)?

**Customer / Account**

**Summary**
Customer proposes creating an agent that checks conversations against a list of preferred terms (terminology points) and reports how consistently reps follow the chosen language.

* Customer: "we go define, like, 10 points of terminology that we care about, and we go see really quickly, like, how how we're doing."

* Intended users initially: internal team; could become customer-facing offering.

**Impact**
Without automated auditing, terminology adherence is hard to measure and inconsistent language persists across customer conversations.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What should the output format be (score, pass/fail, flagged examples, coaching suggestions)?
* Where should results surface (Slack, dashboard, per-call page, etc.)?

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Boulter Industries
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCGZ3XF3JF44JP10R4WJAJZ0](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCGZ3XF3JF44JP10R4WJAJZ0) (2025-12-22)

**Summary**

Customer expects to use the phone app to record mostly in-person meetings. Today, if their calendar is not connected and they "just click record", the recording creates a meeting "not attached to anything" and doesn’t know who they were talking to. This suggests a UX gap for mobile-first, in-person workflows.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Mine, I mean, they're in there, but they're all in person. So I'm gonna be probably using the, uh, phone app anyways to turn that on for a particular meeting."
* Customer: "Otherwise, if you just click record, it's going to create a meeting not attached to anything."
* Customer: "right now when you... record anything from your phone, it doesn't know who you're talking to... Or, like, who the attendees were."

**Context / Details**

* Workflow: in-person meetings recorded via mobile app.
* Current workaround: connect calendar so the event appears and can be selected before recording, associating recording to the right contacts/company.
* Gap: mobile recording without calendar connection lacks association.

**Impact**

Recordings may be harder to find and less usable for summaries and company insights because they aren’t connected to the right company/attendees.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* On mobile, can users select or search a company/contact to associate before starting a recording?
* Can association be prompted after recording if no calendar event exists?
issues.title: Mobile app recording creates meetings not attached to company/attendees without calendar connection

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Boulter Industries
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCGZ3XF3JF44JP10R4WJAJZ0](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCGZ3XF3JF44JP10R4WJAJZ0) (2025-12-22)

**Summary**

Customer raised a privacy control gap: workspace "users" cannot mark calls private. They want to record sensitive meetings (example: 1:1s with manager) but keep them private so others in the workspace cannot access them.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "The last thing I wanted to touch up on is privacy because I know that's super important."
* Customer: "users are not able to mark calls private."
* Customer: "I like my one on ones with my manager recorded. Yeah. But I just marked them private so no one listens to it."

**Context / Details**

* Current concept: meetings appear as public vs private.
* Customer role mentioned: "user".
* Intended workflow: selectively record meetings and restrict access based on sensitivity.

**Impact**

Without private-call controls for non-admin users, customer may avoid recording sensitive meetings, reducing capture and adoption.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Should this be a role permission (workspace setting) that admins can enable for users?
* What should default visibility be for recordings created via mobile app?
issues.title: Allow workspace users to mark meetings/calls as private

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Unknown
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3XCEPQH84KBKGQ](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3XCEPQH84KBKGQ) (2025-12-22)

**Summary**
Customer wants an override path when they disagree with a meeting being marked private/public, including capturing "why" and optionally generating a suggested rule based on transcript + user input.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Let's say that it has a call that is public... and they disagree... They then should have a suggest... like, nope. I want this one private."
* Customer: "I would assume that you would you'd be like, I want this meeting private because and then you have to put in some sort of reason, and then it should work from there."

**Context / Details**

* Discussion raised concern about AI "making up" reasons for why it classified a meeting a certain way.
* Proposed mitigation: require user explanation when disagreeing so the system has grounded input before suggesting a rule.

**Impact**
Without a clear disagreement/override flow, users may not be able to correct misclassifications and trust erodes quickly.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Should the product always require a reason when the user disagrees, or make it optional?
* Should the system suggest a rule only in certain cases (e.g., leave-public vs mark-private)?
issues.title: Privacy agent override flow: when user disagrees with classification, prompt for reason and suggest rule

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Unknown
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD3KHE1AQE3XCEPQH84KBKGQ](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KD3KHE1AQE3XCEPQH84KBKGQ) (2025-12-22)

**Summary**
Customer wants a way to test the privacy agent against past meetings to build trust, and to clearly see which rule(s) caused a meeting to be marked private/public.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "I believe that we should need to be able to test it. So I click on a test button, and it opens up my past calls."
* Customer: "So it's very clear to me as a user that I know which rule, uh, which rules or rules it used to mark it private."

**Context / Details**

* Proposed workflow: click "test" 15 see last \~20 calls + search older calls 15 select a call 15 agent classifies private/public 15 UI shows the rule(s) used.
* Goal stated: "the outcome that we're trying to get is trust."

**Impact**
Without a test mode and rule transparency, users may not trust turning on the privacy agent.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Should test mode support bulk testing (multiple calls) or one call at a time?
* Where should this live (workspace settings vs personal settings)?
issues.title: Privacy agent: add 'Test' mode to run against past calls and show which rule fired

* Source: Customer conversation transcript

**Summary**

Customer described an extremely locked-down cybersecurity environment where installing or deploying additional tools on endpoints is an "absolute no no" and even personal phones are not allowed for 2FA. This suggests a need for a deployment option that can work without endpoint installation or with very strict controls.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "any kind of tool or extra deployment of anything on my computer is a absolute no no."
* Customer: "They don't even allow me to use my own personal phone. They said, here's an old Apple phone"
* Customer: "It is a locked down company."

**Context / Details**

* Customer sells cybersecurity; strict HQ monitoring and device controls.
* Current stack mentioned: Salesforce + Zoom; no Gong/Fathom/notetakers allowed on corporate systems.

**Summary**

Customer reports heavy admin work centered in their Google Docs “presale client summary doc” workflow, including manually grabbing call recordings and adding them into the doc, plus moving transcripts into external AI tools for summaries. They want more information to automatically move into the places it needs to go to increase rep capacity.

**Summary**

Customer says AskElephant sounds valuable for traditional sales orgs, but it doesn’t fit their current workflow because they manage channel partners and don’t use a CRM or maintain a pipeline.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "We actually don't sell our product directly. So in my role, I'm really just managing our channel partners across the country."
* Customer: "We don't even use a CRM... I don't even have a pipeline."
* Customer: "It just really doesn't fit what we do within our company."

**Context / Details**

* Customer’s role is channel partner management; they don’t collect leads or chase deals.
* When on calls with end users, they are present as a subject matter expert and do not own the relationship.
* They explicitly stated they do not use a CRM.

**Impact**

AskElephant’s core value (CRM automation + pipeline/deal support) appears misaligned for teams without a CRM/pipeline, potentially blocking adoption for channel/partner-led orgs.

**Summary**
Customer wants AI to compare recorded in-person calls against their sales process and generate a scorecard (e.g., how often reps build rapport or ask for the business) to identify coaching opportunities.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "AI takes that and compares it to our sales process"
* Customer: "Where how many times do we ask for the business?"
* Customer: "give us a scorecard"

**Context / Details**

* Customer differentiates this from a “script”; they want process/steps adherence.
* Example success metric: "Over his last 30 calls, only 30% of the time, is he building a rapport, or is he asking for the business"
* Current problem: manager believes things are happening but lacks data: "I think it's happening, but I have no idea. So it'd be nice to have that data to show"

**Impact**
Without process-adherence scorecards, manager can’t quantify coaching gaps or trend rep behavior over time.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* How is their sales process defined today (custom steps, checklist, doc, fields)?
* What are the exact scorecard dimensions they want to track?

**Summary**

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Unknown: Where will the glossary live (docs site vs marketing site) and what CMS/stack will be used?
* Unknown: Desired URL structure and schema (e.g., one page vs per-term pages)?

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Parallel
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCQ6JEGNDM4J934CSQ4WJCKJ](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCQ6JEGNDM4J934CSQ4WJCKJ) (2025-12-17)

**Summary**

Customer wants every call to be automatically classified (sales call vs customer call) and then have a short post-call summary automatically posted to the appropriate Slack channel, so they can track what happened without doing anything manually.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "once it reviews the call and it figures out what type of call it is"
* Customer: "If it's a sales call, then it's gonna go to sales call channel... If it's a customer call, then it's gonna go to the CX channel."
* Customer: "after the call, it dumps it in here. So it's like, I know what happened with customers. I know what happened with the sales call."

**Context / Details**

* Customer wants the system to "look at the call" and decide what type it is.
* They explicitly want automatic routing to different channels based on that classification.
* They asked for CX summaries "in like three bullet points" after customer calls.

**Impact**

Without reliable auto-routing, customer lacks operational visibility into sales vs customer conversations and has to manually check.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What are the exact Slack channel IDs/names to post into for sales vs CX?
* Should sales calls also be summarized in 3 bullets, or a different format?
issues.title: Slack: route post-call summaries to different channels based on call type (sales vs customer)

**Summary**

Customer expected demo workflows to run automatically, but current automation depended on a meeting naming convention that they don't have. They want AskElephant to detect demos via HubSpot meeting type ("demo discovery") rather than relying on meeting title naming.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "I don't want anything to be manual."
* Customer: "And we call it a different different naming convention."
* Customer: "So there's not a naming convention for the demos."

**Context / Details**

* Workflow previously used manual triggers; an attempt was made to switch to automatic based on an assumed naming convention.
* Customer indicates demo meetings can be identified in HubSpot via meeting type = "demo discovery".
* They want this to work without needing to standardize meeting naming.

**Impact**

If automation depends on meeting naming, workflows may not run and product pains/follow-up/deal updates won't be captured automatically.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Are there any other meeting types besides "demo discovery" that should trigger the demo workflow?
issues.title: Automations: remove dependency on meeting naming convention; use HubSpot meeting type instead

* Source: Customer conversation transcript

Summary

* Customer wants coaching/scorecard output that provides a few clear bullet points for reps to read quickly, while also allowing a more detailed, strict rubric breakdown for deeper review and manager QA.

Customer Quote(s)

* Customer: "he wanted just, hey. I just want a couple quick bullet points that they can just read clearly"
* Customer: "Like, we can have both"
* Customer: "then I can dive into the deeper, more, um, strict kind of rubric of of the breakdown of the entire call"

Context / Details

* Intended for a very green sales team; goal is self-coaching and manager coaching without "feature dump".
* They want the rep experience to be lightweight but keep depth available.

Impact

* If output is only verbose/detailed, reps may not adopt; if only high-level, managers lose explainability and coaching specificity.

Open Questions (if needed)

* Where should these two layers live (Slack message vs UI vs HubSpot fields), and how should users toggle between them?
issues.title: Automations: coaching output should support 'quick bullets' + deeper rubric view

issues.description:
Customer / Account

* Company: Boostly
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCPNJD12TGCPTH54ZZMBQPFQ](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCPNJD12TGCPTH54ZZMBQPFQ) (2025-12-17)

Summary

* Customer discussed a workflow where deals with no activity for >1 day should move to a "dirty deal desk" and trigger Slack reminders, with escalation to the rep’s manager after additional time.

Customer Quote(s)

* Customer: "when the last activity date is more than, a day ago, it triggers a different process in our system that puts them on a dirty deal desk"
* Customer: "it also triggers, uh, AskElephant workflow to send them a Slack that says, these are your dirty deals for today"
* Customer: "after a week, it says, hey. You've had four deals on this dirty desk for a week, and I'll send the Slack to that person and their manager"

Context / Details

* Workflow concept references HubSpot "last activity date" and a Slack notification cadence (daily reminder + manager escalation).
* Intended to enforce accountability and deal hygiene for a green sales team.

Impact

* Without a standardized/productized way to do this, RevOps has to build/maintain custom enforcement logic and Slack messaging; risk that accountability workflows are inconsistent.

Open Questions (if needed)

* Should this be supported as a first-class automation template, or is current workflow builder sufficient?
issues.title: Slack alerts for 'dirty deals' / inactivity escalation (last activity > 1 day)

**Summary**

Customer wants AEs to stop manually typing pain points and call details, and instead have AskElephant provide a key summary that can be used as a cleaner handoff to activation managers so onboarding can be more prescriptive and avoid re-asking the same questions.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "the AEs, I don't wanna have them typing in pain points and what we talked about. Just a key summary"
* Customer: "the activation manager could be much more prescriptive in delivering a solid onboarding instead of asking the blast the same questions we've been asking"

**Context / Details**

* Workflow spans SDR 12 AE 12 activation manager.
* They want consistent context continuity across the customer journey.

**Impact**

Today, activation managers risk missing context and repeating discovery because key details are manually captured (or not captured) by AEs.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Where should the "key summary" live (CRM record, AskElephant customer profile, recap email, etc.)?
* What fields/sections are required for activation to run a prescriptive onboarding?

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: JellyFish Lighting
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCM31Z32GQNBZNHK75TD8TT7](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCM31Z32GQNBZNHK75TD8TT7) (2025-12-17)

**Summary**

JellyFish Lighting wants AskElephant to integrate directly with their calling system (Callingly) so call transcripts (and potentially AI summaries) can flow into AskElephant for coaching analysis at scale. They mentioned Callingly may be able to send transcripts via Zapier/events, but AskElephant currently lacks a direct integration.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Currently, we don't have a direct integration with Callingly, so that's something that we would have to build for you."
* Customer: "I think the transcript, if that could event could go to where you need it to be. They're at least able to, uh, expose that."
* Customer: "Is there a source that we could get start with without having all of the data sort? Like, what if it was just callingly or whatever?"

**Context / Details**

* Customer stack includes Callingly (calls), Keap (CRM), Slack, Gmail; GoToConnect is used for office admins; Fix Your Funnel is used for texting.
* They referenced that Callingly has built integrations with "Gong and Silkscreen" (unclear spelling) and can likely send out transcripts / AI summary.
* Primary use case: automated coaching across \~10,000 calls/month.

**Impact**

Without a Callingly integration or supported ingestion path (e.g., via Zapier/webhooks), the customer can’t easily get call conversation data into AskElephant to run coaching workflows at scale.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What Callingly data is available via API/webhooks/Zapier (audio, transcript, AI summary, metadata like duration/agent id)?
* Does AskElephant need audio, transcript, or both for their coaching workflows?
* What identifiers can be used to map calls to reps/teams and (optionally) CRM records?

Classification: type/feature-request
Product Area Label: area/integrations-(general)
Project: Integrations (General)
Milestone: New Integration Requests
Priority: Medium
issues.title: Callingly integration request (transcripts/AI summary via Zapier/events)

Customer asked for an admin-friendly way to automatically prevent recording for meetings involving certain customer domains (example: banking customer), instead of manually toggling per meeting.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "I see the domain for one of my customers. That's a banking customer. Is there a way to, hey. If I email this particular domain, those meetings never get recorded, for instance."
* Customer: "if I know this is a particular client that I don't want it to join"

**Context / Details**

* Customer is cautious about accidentally recording meetings where clients have 'no record' policies.
* Current workaround is manual per-meeting controls (toggle recording / mark private).
* They want rules based on attendee email domain.

**Impact**
Risk of compliance issues and customer trust loss if a restricted-domain meeting is recorded by default; adds overhead for teams to manage recording settings.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Should rules be workspace-wide, per-user, or both?
* Should rule action be "do not join", "join but do not record", or "auto-mark private"?
issues.title: Domain-based recording/privacy rules (e.g., don't record meetings with specific customer domains)

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Parakeet Financial
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3B532/engagements/ngmt_01KC29C81XWW1FF76FH5H8MM3T](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3B532/engagements/ngmt_01KC29C81XWW1FF76FH5H8MM3T) (2025-12-17)

**Summary**

Customer runs client onboarding via a manual Word document template to capture ICP and other constraints from kickoff calls, then uses AI output to hand off to an internal owner to write SDR call scripts. They explicitly want to automate this template-driven intake.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "this is kind of a working it's it's a word word document that we're we're trying to automate as well"
* Customer: "It's collecting their ICP... if there's geographic restrictions or exclusions"
* Customer: "word doc that as of right now is being recorded with, like, a Fathom... and then pushing pushed into, like, an AI output"

**Context / Details**

* Workflow: onboarding kickoff call → fill Word doc ICP template → meeting recorded with Fathom/recorder → AI output generated → handed to "sales training and development" to write call scripts → iterate based on what works.
* Automation goal: reduce manual doc work and streamline handoff inputs for scripting and outbound execution.

**Impact**

Manual template completion adds admin overhead and slows time-to-launch for new client outbound programs.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Where should the structured onboarding output live (AskElephant customer profile, Slack, Notion, Google Doc)?
* Do they want the system to generate initial call scripts directly from the intake?
issues.title: Automate onboarding 'Word doc ICP template' capture from kickoff calls

**Summary**
Customer requests pre-loaded demo calls (e.g., \~20 example calls across common scenarios) so new users can immediately see how AskElephant supports key workflows without needing to import data or already have history in the system.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "You need demo calls in the platform."

* Success criteria implied: users can explore templates/coaching/handoffs/product insights on sample data immediately after signup.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "they were only analyzing 45 to 60 calls per day"
* Customer: "It is only analyzing 10% of of the calls that we are we were expecting to analyze."
* Customer: "AskElephant was only reading the transcripts out of 60 or 45 calls or 17 calls, for example, yesterday."

**Context / Details**

* Workflow: "daily BDR call report" sending Slack messages with analysis.
* Source system: HubSpot where "recordings and the transcripts of those calls are logged".
* Volume change: worked well initially at \~100–150 calls/day; issue appeared when volume increased to \~500–600 calls/day.
* No minimum call duration filter appears set currently (per internal check during call).
* Dialer: Trellis; calls should be in HubSpot.
* Some calls are analyzed and some are not; customer offered to provide daily report examples showing mismatch.

**Impact**
Customer cannot rely on AskElephant for full-coverage analysis of their outbound program and reverted to another workflow tool to analyze all calls.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What determines which HubSpot call transcripts are included/excluded in the workflow execution?
* Are there rate limits, batching, pagination, or time window constraints causing partial ingestion/processing at higher volumes?
* Can customer share specific examples of calls not analyzed vs analyzed (HubSpot record IDs/links)?
issues.title: Daily BDR call report only analyzes ~45–60 of ~450–600 HubSpot calls

**Summary**

Customer indicated their org is rolling out Zoho (implementation + quoting) and that AskElephant not integrating with Zoho makes the product "probably not" a fit. They’d like to get to a place where they can use tools like AskElephant but current CRM stack blocks adoption.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "So Zoho is another, like, z CRM, but we're using that on, like, the implementation side. And then we're gonna start doing our quoting from there for sales"
* Customer: "eventually, we could go everything to Zoho"
* Customer: "But you would have to be moving away from Zoho. Like, we don't integrate with Zoho."

**Context / Details**

* They use HubSpot today but are rolling out Zoho and starting quoting in Zoho in the New Year
* They may keep HubSpot for marketing/sales activity, but they’re unsure why they’d keep HubSpot if everything else moves to Zoho
* Decision risk: adopting AskElephant would require standardizing on HubSpot; customer suggested this likely won’t happen

**Impact**

AskElephant is not adoptable for this customer if Zoho becomes their system of record for implementation/sales quoting.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Which Zoho products/modules are in use (Zoho CRM, Zoho Desk, Zoho Creator, etc.)?
* Would they need bi-directional sync or just activity logging / field updates from AskElephant?
* Is HubSpot expected to remain in place long-term or be replaced entirely?
issues.title: Zoho integration needed (current blocker to adopting AskElephant)

**Summary**
Customer said they have limited appetite to implement new tools because they’re early in Gong implementation. This points to a need for a clearer phased/low-lift onboarding path so AskElephant can be evaluated without feeling like another heavy rollout.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "We're pretty early in the implementation of Gong, so our appetite for implementing new things is probably pretty limited right now."

**Context / Details**

* Existing tool initiative: Gong rollout in progress
* CRM mentioned: HubSpot
* AskElephant was described as: autonomous HubSpot updates (no copy/paste) + Slack coaching insights + integrates with Gong
* Customer indicated they can review "standard kind of overview information" later, but not invest time now

**Impact**
Prospects may delay or decline evaluation if AskElephant onboarding feels heavy or duplicative during a Gong rollout, even when incremental value exists.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What is the minimum viable setup for a “Gong already in-flight” customer (time-to-value target, required permissions, required integrations)?
* Can we support an explicitly phased rollout (e.g., start with Slack insights only, then enable HubSpot updates)?

* Classification: type/feature-request
* Product Area: area/integrations-(general)
* Project: Onboarding & Activation
* Milestone: Workspace Setup
* Priority (best-guess): Medium

**Summary**
Customer-facing messaging in this outreach describes AskElephant as a "platform for billing automated agents" while also promising pipeline visibility and 10–15% conversion lift. Product needs to confirm/standardize the correct positioning language to avoid confusion.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "We’re a platform for billing automated agents, which are like digital assistants"
* Customer: "They can help you get more visibility in your pipeline and increase conversion rates 10 to 15%"

**Context / Details**

* This was a voicemail left while attempting to reach Connor Rickman via Danielle.
* The message mixes billing automation language with sales pipeline/conversion outcomes.

**Impact**
Risk of confusing prospects about what the product does, reducing demo conversion and creating mismatched expectations.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Is "billing automated agents" accurate for our current product scope? If not, what is the approved language?
* What proof points are approved for conversion lift claims (and in what contexts)?
issues.title: Clarify product positioning: 'billing automated agents' vs pipeline visibility/conversion claims

**Summary**
Customer is currently using Teams recording/transcripts inconsistently and is "not really impressed" with what Teams provides. They want AskElephant to deliver a better call recording + transcript experience when meetings are on Microsoft Teams.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Not really impressed with what Teams is providing."
* Customer: "...provide an improved call recording experience with sales AI enablement..."

**Context / Details**

* Environment: Microsoft Teams meetings.

Field Mapping

**Summary**

Customer indicated their current tooling is already connected and they have visibility, implying AskElephant’s value vs existing tools (Gong/Clari) wasn’t compelling for their setup. This suggests we may need clearer product differentiation and/or integration-health messaging for teams with mature RevTech stacks.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "We're using Gong. What's the other one? We have Gong. We have Clari. We have a lot we have, like, 60 apps in our stack here."
* Customer: "I'm good right now. I haven't seen any like, everything for me is pretty much connected. I have visibility for all of that."
* Customer: "I'm probably the wrong I'm more or less the wrong target for this product or service."

**Context / Details**

* Customer works "on the channel side" and said they don't directly deal with sales team tooling.
* They reported a large app stack and stated their tools are connected with adequate visibility.
* Our pitch referenced HubSpot/Salesforce "second brain" + autonomous CRM updates tied to custom fields; customer did not engage on CRM pain.

**Impact**

Harder to win or even schedule discovery with prospects who believe their current stack (e.g., Gong + Clari) already solves integration/visibility, especially outside direct Sales leadership personas.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Unknown: Which CRM (HubSpot vs Salesforce) are they using?
* Unknown: What specific "visibility" dashboards/workflows do they rely on today (Clari, Gong, CRM reports, etc.)?
* Unknown: Are there any gaps in CRM field population or handoff consistency they still experience?
issues.title: Prospect says stack is already connected (Gong + Clari) — unclear differentiation vs existing tools

**Summary**

Customer is unclear how workflow execution usage is attributed and billed when one person builds workflows that run for the broader organization. They want clarity on whether automated workflow token usage counts against the workflow creator, a specific user, or the overall company/workspace.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "how do you do workflows?"
* Customer: "who gets credit for those that token usage that's, like, happening automatedly?"
* Customer: "do those go in my name, or do those just go against the general Rivet?"

**Context / Details**

* Scenario described: many users ("70") but fewer paid users ("34, 35 paid users").
* RevOps person creates workflows used across the team, potentially including heavy analytical/reporting usage.

**Impact**

Unclear usage attribution creates friction in adopting workflows at scale and makes it hard to forecast cost and decide who needs a paid license.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Do workflows always run 9as a specific user, or can they run under a workspace/service account?
* Is there an admin view to see workflow usage by workflow, by owner, and by beneficiaries?
* Can billing be separated for workflow execution vs interactive chat usage?
issues.title: Workflow token/usage attribution, who is billed when workflows run for shared org use

**Summary**
Customer wants the ability to keep certain client call data stored "stateside" (and potentially in a specific place) for enterprise/security reasons, while other calls could be stored elsewhere.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Low stateside?"
* Customer: "it stays stateside"
* Customer: "you could run our calls through one of those servers and it stays stateside. It stays, you know, encrypted and transit encrypted at rest"

**Context / Details**

* Customer notes there are some clients they "just can't record" today.
* They described an internal approach: spin up an Amazon server, keep everything internal, use models that do not train on their data, and allow switching between models.
* They want the ability to "take data and store it specific places" for upstream enterprise needs.

**Impact**
Without controllable data residency/storage location, enterprise customers or certain clients may block recording/AI processing, limiting adoption.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Which data types must be stateside (audio, video, transcript, embeddings, metadata)?
* Is this per-workspace, per-company, per-meeting, or per-attendee policy?
* What are the acceptable storage regions/providers (AWS US regions only, customer-managed VPC, etc.)?
issues.title: Support 'stateside' data storage for recordings/transcripts (enterprise request)

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Unknown
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* 

**Summary**
Customer needs a fast way to recreate a small set of legacy website URLs during a site migration so partner bots (Zoom, HubSpot) can verify the pages still exist. They explicitly want “good enough” pages quickly, not pixel-perfect design.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "the point is not to make these look pretty"
* Customer: "Zoom, like, has a bot that checks to make sure we have this page on our site"
* Customer: "We just need them to exist"

**Context / Details**

* Migrating from an old sitemap/URL set to a new website.
* At least five specific pages/URLs need to be recreated quickly (including a Zoom partners download page and a HubSpot-related page).
* Guidance is to time-box creation to \~10 minutes per page and use old site content as reference.
* Discussion suggests these pages may not need to be linked in the footer/navigation as long as they are accessible for automated checks.

**Impact**
If these legacy URLs don’t exist after migration, Zoom/HubSpot automated verification may fail and create partner/compliance issues.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* What are the exact five URLs that must be recreated?
* Are there specific HTTP status/SEO requirements (e.g., 200 vs redirect) for the partner bots?

---

Classification: type/feature-request
Product Area: area/website
Project: Website
Milestone: SEO
Priority (best-guess): High
issues.title: Website migration: need placeholder pages that 'just exist' for Zoom/HubSpot bot checks
