Notion integration description claims it will “update it in real time,” but participants called out that AskElephant doesn’t have live updates yet. This could set the wrong expectation (e.g., writing into Notion during a call).

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: “on Notion, it says update it in real time… because we don't have, like, live updates yet.”
* Customer: “if someone were to say, oh, so while I'm on a call, it'll be writing notes in Notion.”

**Context / Details**

* Discussion was reviewing website integration descriptions for accuracy.
* Concern is specifically around Notion wording implying in-call/live note writing inside a Notion doc.

**Impact**

Misleading copy may cause users to expect live Notion note writing and be disappointed when behavior is not supported.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Should we update Notion copy to describe current behavior (e.g., post-call export) vs. “real-time”?
issues.title: Notion integration copy says “update it in real time” but we don’t have live updates

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: gmail.cojm
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCJ39WKP4J1FVEFBEX4B59P2](https://app.askelephant.ai/workspaces/wrks_01JN30KSYSBMDRRVA3F62N2GHW/engagements/ngmt_01KCJ39WKP4J1FVEFBEX4B59P2) (2025-12-15)

**Summary**

Customer asked whether AskElephant can “take it a step further” than Gong, specifically around what gets pushed into Salesforce automatically (including custom fields) and what still requires manual steps. This needs product clarification to avoid mismatched expectations on Salesforce field updates and automation depth.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "Gong updates inside of Salesforce for that opportunity"
* Customer: "it will generate that in custom fields inside of Salesforce today. Are you seeing that AskElephant can take it a step further than that?"
* Customer: "If that truly is the case ... it's analyzing Gong calls ... and then automatically pushing updates into Salesforce"

**Context / Details**

* Current Gong workflow: after calls, Gong populates per-call fields (agenda, participants, outcomes) into Salesforce.
* Customer is evaluating whether AskElephant auto-populates additional fields/insights and/or aggregates across calls without prompting.
* Risk: ambiguity in which fields are written, where they are written (Opportunity vs Activity vs custom objects), and whether any user action is required.

**Impact**

* Unclear capabilities could create implementation friction and adoption issues if promised “automatic” updates aren’t fully automatic or don’t map to their custom fields.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Exactly which Salesforce fields/objects can AskElephant write to today (standard + custom)?
* Does AskElephant require any user action (button press, prompt, selection) to trigger writes?
* When integrating with Gong, what is the division of responsibility (what comes from Gong vs AskElephant)?
issues.title: Clarify/validate what “automatically pushing updates into Salesforce” includes vs Gong

**Summary**

* Customer: "at the end of the day, it's gonna be a task"

**Context / Details**

**Customer / Account**

* Company: Enfos Software
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript

**Summary**

When the customer initiates the Microsoft connection but then exits the consent screen (because their internal approval is not complete), returning to the app sends them to an error page. They saw the same behavior during testing the prior week.

**Customer Quote(s)**

* Customer: "If I start the connection to connect Microsoft... I get blocked here because our approval isn't complete yet. But if I return to application without granting consent, I'm taken to an error page"
* Customer: "return without connecting is throwing up"
* Customer: "we were testing it last week and got the same error too"

**Context / Details**

* Flow: logged into AskElephant → start Microsoft connect → reach Microsoft consent/approval step (customer cannot complete due to admin approval not done) → click "return to application" without granting consent → error page
* Customer notes bug is not currently preventing them from using AskElephant, but it is a rough edge during setup

**Impact**

Creates a broken/unclear experience during Microsoft integration setup, increasing onboarding friction and support load.

**Open Questions (if needed)**

* Unknown: exact error page content/status code
* Unknown: does this occur for other OAuth integrations or only Microsoft
issues.title: Microsoft connection OAuth flow: 'Return to application without granting consent' leads to error page

**Summary** - The customer currently lacks an automated process for generating sales-to-Account Manager (AM) handoff documents, leading to critical information being scattered across Salesforce, emails, chats, and call transcripts, and often getting lost.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "So it it there is no document that would be generated anywhere. It's it's a combination of data points we capture in Salesforce. Most of it and some information still, unfortunately, keeps sitting in emails, chats, and call transcripts. So it gets lost somewhere in the handover and does not get passed from the AE to AM."

**Context / Details** - The current manual process relies on AEs to remember and document all details from 5-10 calls in a sales cycle, resulting in missed information during handoffs. The customer desires an automated solution to consolidate these data points.

**Impact** - Critical customer expectations and requirements are lost during handoffs, leading to less effective onboarding for AMs and potentially impacting customer satisfaction and retention. This is a significant operational inefficiency.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: FEATURE REQUEST: Automated Sales-to-AM Handoff Document Workflow

**Context / Details** - The customer expressed surprise and frustration that AskElephant does not offer a free trial, stating that free trials are standard in the market. AskElephant typically offers paid 30-day pilots to cover overhead.

**Summary** - The customer perceives AskElephant's business model, particularly the distinction and pricing for "viewer" versus "customer-facing" user tiers, as unclear and still evolving, making it difficult to confidently plan license allocation and budget.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Mike: "So in terms of customer facing, we're we're figuring this out. We're gonna be releasing a new iteration, more clarity on this." Customer: "So And the reason we're not we're talking about 30% discount is I we are not the first company where where you have a small group of people that wants to record, and you have a larger group of people who want to just view a video once in a while. And they're not gonna make your so if I understand it right, you're still evolving or figuring out that business model. Right? How to accommodate that?"

**Context / Details** - The customer (and an internal AE) acknowledged that the current explanation of tiered pricing ($100 for customer-facing, $25 for non-customer-facing/viewer with limited agents) is complex and not fully defined, especially when considering scenarios like a RevOps person building agents for other teams.

**Impact** - Ambiguity in the pricing model creates difficulty for customers in understanding the true cost and value proposition, hindering budget approvals and making it challenging for them to allocate licenses effectively. This can be a barrier to commitment.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: QUESTION: Clarify evolving business model for viewer vs. customer-facing tiers

**Summary** - The customer received conflicting information from an SDR regarding the availability of free viewer licenses, leading to confusion about AskElephant's licensing model. They noted that competitors offer free trials.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "I also heard from other Relux colleague that you guys actually have a free viewer license, which then threw me off a little bit. That's why I was then also explicitly asking because it's wasn't consistent with what you told me. Then I challenged back him a little bit. And he said, oh, I was just talking to SDR, and that's what the SDR told me. So now it turns out that you guys don't have a viewer license. Right? Everybody who wants to browse through recorded calls needs an AskElephant paid license at least the essential seat."

**Context / Details** - The customer was told by an SDR that AskElephant offered free viewer licenses, which contradicts the actual pricing model requiring at least an "Essential seat" ($25/month) for anyone accessing the platform. The customer also contrasted this with competitors offering free trials.

**Impact** - Misleading information creates confusion, erodes trust, and leads to unexpected costs for customers who assume free viewer access. This can negatively impact sales and adoption.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: IMPROVEMENT: Clarify viewer license model and address SDR miscommunication

**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KBDCYT97ZTKE2VDNVRR3Y88E](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KBDCYT97ZTKE2VDNVRR3Y88E) (Dec 1 2025)

**Summary** - The customer was informed that AskElephant charges $0.60/hr for historical call data migration from Mojo, in addition to the subscription cost, which is a concern given their existing contract with Mojo and the need to import 1500 calls.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer (paraphrased from Pete's response): "He did mention some fees associated with that, so so that has been clarified." Pete: "the cost for pulling the data from Modjo will be $0.60/hr for the calls. This will allow us to transcribe and run the software across those calls."

**Context / Details** - CHILI publish needs to migrate approximately 1500 calls (around 2022 hours) from Mojo AI. The cost for this migration is $0.60 per hour of recording. The customer notes their Mojo license costs 5.7k annually and sees additional migration fees as a hurdle.

**Impact** - The additional cost for data migration adds to the overall expense of switching to AskElephant, potentially making it less compelling compared to competitors or simply renewing Mojo, which could be a factor in churn.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: PRODUCT GAP: Cost for historical data migration from Mojo

issues.description:
**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC24Q9WVYW2R6HQ1M4KK3M0S](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC24Q9WVYW2R6HQ1M4KK3M0S) (Dec 9 2025)

**Summary** - The customer wants AskElephant to automatically build context from emails that are pushed from AskElephant to an external mail client (like Outlook) and then sent, similar to how it would pull information from CRM.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "So if I build it here and I push it to my Outlook client and then I send it, does that build context for AskElephant? Erika: No. Not right now, unfortunately. But, yeah, we can push it from AskElephant, but it won't get previous email context. Unless it's under CRM, then it can look in Salesforce."

**Context / Details** - Currently, emails drafted and sent through external mail clients (even if initiated from AskElephant) do not contribute to AskElephant's context building. The AI can pull some context from CRM notes, but not directly from external email activities unless they are explicitly logged as Salesforce activity objects (which is another feature request).

**Impact** - Limits the AI's ability to maintain a holistic view of customer communications, potentially missing valuable context for churn signals or overall customer interaction history.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: FEATURE REQUEST: Bidirectional email context sync for external clients

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "I did invite my team to AskElephant. What seems to happen is nobody of them actually got any invitation email Not in their normal inbox, not in the junk folder. When I shared the login page with them, they were able to use the email address and then get a verification email. So the second one they got I don't know why the first one invitation, they never got. Maybe something you you guys want to check on, and we are using Microsoft."

**Context / Details** - The customer experienced issues with initial team invitation emails not reaching recipients, despite subsequent verification emails being received. This happened with users on Microsoft email systems.

**Impact** - Creates significant friction during team onboarding, delays user adoption, and requires manual workarounds, leading to a frustrating initial experience with the platform.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: BUG: Team invitation emails are not delivered

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "If we decide to leave AskElephant after a year. Mhmm. And we also get exported calls from your platform, or is there an API access that a different provider could then use to get the recordings?" "Please do. I know that I plan to terminate if we if we go with you guys, but you don't know. Right? And Yeah. But this is a good question."

**Impact** - Lack of a bulk data export option via API creates a data portability concern, which could be a barrier for customers considering long-term commitment or churn scenarios.

issues.title: FEATURE REQUEST: API for exporting call recordings

issues.description:
**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W) (Dec 10 2025)

**Summary** - The process for manually importing calls and managing associated contacts and companies is inefficient, requiring users to manually connect each contact one by one and lacking the ability to create new contacts directly from the company edit screen.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "When I go to meetings, because now I'm doing manual upload. So I have to manually connect companies and contacts. I cannot add more contacts at once. I need to go one by one By one. Then as I go and clean up companies, I was importing Hilton data, I can go into edit company. But when I go to add contact, I can only search for contacts that already exist. I cannot add a new contact here from the screen. So I can say Erica is with Hilton, but I can't create Tyler."

**Context / Details** - During manual call uploads, users must individually associate contacts and companies. Furthermore, when editing a company, there's no option to create a new contact; users can only search existing ones. This forces a roundabout workflow (create contact elsewhere, then associate).

**Impact** - The process is tedious and time-consuming, especially for large imports or when dealing with new contacts not yet in the system. This creates significant friction during data cleanup and organization.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: UX: Inefficient manual call import and contact/company management

**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W) (Dec 10 2025)

**Summary** - The customer is questioning the current limitation of searching past meetings to a fixed 90-day window and asks if it's possible to have a dynamic date range selection.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "there seems to be a limitation on the search notes. Search past meeting, I can only go up to last ninety days, and I cannot select the frame dynamically as in last twenty days. Again, is this on purpose? Is this can I bypass this somehow?"

**Context / Details** - The system currently restricts historical meeting searches to a 90-day lookback, without options for custom date ranges. Erika explained this is to prevent AI hallucination with too much context. The customer acknowledges this and suggests manual dataset creation as a workaround.

**Impact** - Limits flexibility in analyzing historical data for specific timeframes beyond the fixed 90-day window.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: QUESTION: Dynamic date range for historical meeting search

**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W) (Dec 10 2025)

**Summary** - The customer notes that a competitor (Gemini) offers a deeper Salesforce integration where their conversational intelligence platform is embedded as an iframe within Salesforce, allowing AE/AMs to stay in their CRM for full functionality. The customer questions if AskElephant plans to offer similar functionality.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "They one of their selling points is full integration with Salesforce in a form where they basically ice cream their platform into Salesforce. So that you as an AEAM don't need to lead the CRM. You you kind of have your whole conversational intelligence embedded in the CRM."

**Context / Details** - This is a comparison to a competitor's offering that provides a more seamless user experience by integrating directly into the Salesforce UI, reducing context switching.

**Impact** - AskElephant's current Salesforce integration, while functional, requires users to switch between platforms to fully leverage conversational intelligence, which is less efficient compared to embedded solutions.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: FEATURE REQUEST: Deeper Salesforce UI integration (e.g., iframe)

issues.description:
**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC4GCVPVACA8G904V47TDH5W) (Dec 10 2025)

**Summary** - The Salesforce agent feature requires manual enablement via a feature flag for each user, rather than being automatically available for all premium users who need the integration.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "The Salesforce agent that you guys enabled for me yesterday, that's not something that was enabled for the rest of the team. Right? That was just for me. ... I mean, in if if we move to a production use, I think everybody will be building also their own workflows. Right? Or will be asking for it. So we have seven full premium users, so those will be my revenue people, and they would need the Salesforce integration."

**Context / Details** - The Salesforce agent, a key integration feature, is currently behind a feature flag and needs to be enabled individually, even for users who are on a premium tier and require it for their roles.

**Impact** - Adds friction to the onboarding and setup process for teams needing Salesforce integration. Requires manual intervention from support or internal admins to enable it for all relevant users, delaying full team adoption.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: ONBOARDING: Salesforce agent feature flag not enabled by default for premium users

**Summary** - The 'Create conversation' node in the workflow builder generates random names for conversations if the user does not explicitly provide a title, leading to confusion and unintuitive behavior.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "Why do you push a random name? Like, it's why why doesn't the the the create conversation note force me to give a name?"

**Context / Details** - When configuring the 'create conversation' node, if the title field is left blank, AskElephant automatically assigns a random name to the conversation.

* Reproduction hint: Create a workflow with a 'Create conversation' node and leave the title field blank. Run the workflow.

**Impact** - Users are confused by randomly named conversations. They have to delete the randomly named conversation and rerun the workflow after adding a title. This is unintuitive and creates friction.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: Workflow Builder: Random conversation names if title not provided

**Customer Quote(s)** - Mike: "So, yes, we have released it. Mhmm. I think it is out of beta. That being said, I do want to double check with my team, make sure it has all the functionality. I haven't overpromised anything."

**Context / Details** - The customer was initially informed of a 1-2 month timeline for Salesforce integration. Upon hearing it was released, there was still a need for internal verification on its completeness and robustness compared to other CRM integrations like HubSpot.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "If you shared it with me, I am able to access this particular call. Do I have also access to the AI functionalities? No? Okay. Okay. Okay. You got it." (Referring to a shared meeting link)

**Context / Details** - Sharing recordings with external stakeholders or internal team members who do not have an AskElephant seat only provides basic video viewing. AI features and insights are restricted to logged-in users with appropriate licenses.

**Impact** - Limits the ability to demonstrate the full value of AskElephant's AI capabilities to a wider audience (e.g., leadership, partners, or other departments) without requiring them to purchase a full seat. This could hinder broader buy-in and adoption.

**Summary** - AskElephant can pull notes from Salesforce, but not historical email activity stored in custom objects linked to accounts, contacts, or leads, limiting the AI's ability to build a comprehensive customer context.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "You say notes. So it won't be able to get activities from Salesforce that would be the actual emails? Where do they need the activities live? ... It's a separate object linked to either account contact or a lead."

**Context / Details** - The customer's Salesforce instance stores emails as activity objects related to various records. AskElephant currently cannot access this type of historical email data for context building.

**Impact** - The AI's ability to provide a complete view of customer interactions and build comprehensive context (e.g., for churn signals) is limited without access to historical email communications within Salesforce. This requires product follow-up to clarify integration capabilities.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: FEATURE REQUEST: Salesforce email activity object integration for context

**Summary** - The customer perceives AskElephant's business model, particularly the distinction and pricing for "viewer" versus "customer-facing" user tiers, as unclear and still evolving, making it difficult to confidently plan license allocation and budget.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Mike: "So in terms of customer facing, we're we're figuring this out. We're gonna be releasing a new iteration, more clarity on this." Customer: "So And the reason we're not we're talking about 30% discount is I we are not the first company where where you have a small group of people that wants to record, and you have a larger group of people who want to just view a video once in a while. And they're not gonna make your so if I understand it right, you're still evolving or figuring out that business model. Right? How to accommodate that?"

**Context / Details** - The customer (and an internal AE) acknowledged that the current explanation of tiered pricing ($100 for customer-facing, $25 for non-customer-facing/viewer with limited agents) is complex and not fully defined, especially when considering scenarios like a RevOps person building agents for other teams.

**Impact** - Ambiguity in the pricing model creates difficulty for customers in understanding the true cost and value proposition, hindering budget approvals and making it challenging for them to allocate licenses effectively. This can be a barrier to commitment.

**Open Questions (if needed)** - None
issues.title: QUESTION: Clarify evolving business model for viewer vs. customer-facing tiers

**Customer / Account** - Company: CHILI publish - Account Status: Churned - Segment/Tier: Unknown - Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback) - Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KB0G6B2YTKXAYG6BHZVDQPGT](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KB0G6B2YTKXAYG6BHZVDQPGT) (Nov 26 2025)

**Summary** - The customer received conflicting information from an SDR regarding the availability of free viewer licenses, leading to confusion about AskElephant's licensing model. They noted that competitors offer free trials.

**Customer Quote(s)** - Customer: "I also heard from other Relux colleague that you guys actually have a free viewer license, which then threw me off a little bit. That's why I was then also explicitly asking because it's wasn't consistent with what you told me. Then I challenged back him a little bit. And he said, oh, I was just talking to SDR, and that's what the SDR told me. So now it turns out that you guys don't have a viewer license. Right? Everybody who wants to browse through recorded calls needs an AskElephant paid license at least the essential seat."

**Context / Details** - The customer was told by an SDR that AskElephant offered free viewer licenses, which contradicts the actual pricing model requiring at least an "Essential seat" ($25/month) for anyone accessing the platform. The customer also contrasted this with competitors offering free trials.

**Impact** - Misleading information creates confusion, erodes trust, and leads to unexpected costs for customers who assume free viewer access. This can negatively impact sales and adoption.

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Sendoso
* Account Status: Churned
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback)
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC7TT4NSYJTTF8W1VVZFAVQV](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC7TT4NSYJTTF8W1VVZFAVQV) (Dec 11, 2025); [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC56FPTVMFENWPKAF0D81HZ5](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC56FPTVMFENWPKAF0D81HZ5) (Dec 10, 2025)
  **Summary**
  Customers desire a more deterministic and user-controlled method for updating Salesforce fields via AI, including the ability to directly select specific fields and a granular human-in-the-loop mechanism to review and approve changes before they are pushed to Salesforce.
  **Customer Quote(s)**
* Customer: "our next version of this is going to allow for deterministic, meaning you can select the actual field Mhmm. Instead of having to, like, do the double curly bracket and say, like, reference this."
* Customer: "It should be an option, um, on each individual field or workflow or whatever. Whoever is creating the workflow should be able to say, this requires human in the loop. This one does not."
* Customer: "it should be the proposed updates go to the rep or whoever the human is involved. They have to review and approve before it goes to Salesforce."
  **Context / Details**
  Egan Callahan expressed that current AI-driven field population lacks direct mapping capabilities, relying on probabilistic LLM interpretation. He suggests implementing a feature where users can explicitly select fields and have control over which fields require human approval before being updated in Salesforce.
  **Impact**
  Without deterministic control and granular human-in-the-loop review, there's a risk of inaccurate "AI slop" entering the CRM, undermining trust and potentially leading to data quality issues.
  **Open Questions (if needed)**
  Unknown

issues.description:
**Customer / Account**

* Company: Sendoso
* Account Status: Churned
* Segment/Tier: Unknown
* Source: Customer conversation transcript (churn feedback)
* Meeting: [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC56FPTVMFENWPKAF0D81HZ5](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC56FPTVMFENWPKAF0D81HZ5) (Dec 10, 2025); [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC7TT4NSYJTTF8W1VVZFAVQV](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01KC7TT4NSYJTTF8W1VVZFAVQV) (Dec 11, 2025); [https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01K9DDWEAQGPJG6AERGZBX6KRY](https://app.askelephant.ai/share/clips/mclp_01K9DDWEAQGPJG6AERGZBX6KRY) (Nov 6, 2025)
  **Summary**
  Customers are concerned about the use of probabilistic AI (LLMs) for deterministic tasks like updating Salesforce fields, as it can lead to inaccurate data ("AI slop") and a lack of control over critical CRM operations. This concern contributes to their perception that the product is not ready for their needs.
  **Customer Quote(s)**
* Customer: "He's like, really, like, what if it does something wrong? ... it concerned me that we couldn't directly map and take any of the probabilistic nature of AI out of that specific piece."
* Customer: "not having a, uh, better human in the loop mechanism to review that information before it goes into Salesforce."
* Customer: "We are starting a pilot with Momentum, if you've heard of them. ... So to me, it it feels like the product's just not ready yet for for what we're trying to do."
* Customer: "We can't have everybody writing it in the same fields. Otherwise, that's Yeah. It'll screw it up."
  **Context / Details**
  The customer, Sendoso's Go-to-market Engineer Egan Callahan, expressed significant apprehension regarding AskElephant's reliance on LLMs to populate Salesforce fields. He highlighted that field mapping is a deterministic task, and using a probabilistic technology like AI for this leads to potential "AI slop" and lack of trust in the data. This also extended to concerns about concurrent changes from multiple sources overwriting data. They are piloting a competitor (Momentum) due to these product readiness concerns.
  **Impact**
  Risk of inaccurate data in Salesforce, lack of trust in automated CRM updates, increased manual review burden, inability to implement critical workflows reliably, and customer churn to competitors.
  **Open Questions (if needed)**
  Unknown
issues.title: Concern: Probabilistic AI for deterministic Salesforce field updates leading to 'AI slop'

Everybody who wants to browse through recorded calls needs an AskElephant paid license at least the essential seat."

**Context / Details** - The customer was told by an SDR that AskElephant offered free viewer licenses, which contradicts the actual pricing model requiring at least an "Essential seat" ($25/month) for anyone accessing the platform. The customer also contrasted this with competitors offering free trials.

**Impact** - Misleading information creates confusion, erodes trust, and leads to unexpected costs for customers who assume free viewer access. This can negatively impact sales and adoption.

issues.description:
**Requester / Source**

* Requester Name: Customer via Ben Harrison
* Company: Parallel (specific request context)
* Source: Slack channel feedback
* Slack Message Timestamp: 1763663953.082109

**Summary**
A customer requested a method to bulk delete all meetings under 5 min. Currently, they can filter to find these meetings, but there is no way to bulk delete them in the UI. This would help users clear out test/cold-call meetings that clutter their view and data.

**Original Slack Quotes**

* Slack: Ben Harrison: "a customer is asking if we can bulk delete all meetings under 5 min. I can filter to find these but is there a method to bulk delete?"
* Slack: Ben Harrison: "My example is Parallel has dozens of 1 min calls, essentially cold calls not answered and they want them removed."

**Context / Details**

* Parallel (customer) frequently has dozens of very short meetings that clutter their meetings list
* No bulk deletion option in UI; only filtering is possible
* Backend script possible, but not productized/available to users
* This request appears with test calls, cold calls, and accidental meetings

**Impact**

* Customers spend time manually managing meeting lists and cannot efficiently remove unwanted meetings

**Open Questions**

* Would bulk deletion require admin roles?
* Any audit/recoverability needs for deleted meetings?
issues.title: Cannot bulk delete meetings under 5 min

Summary
The customer is confused about AskElephant's licensing model, specifically regarding the need for a paid "essential seat" for users who only want to browse or view recorded calls (like executives, product managers, marketing), as their current tool (Mojo) offers free viewer licenses. They also highlighted a discrepancy where an SDR mentioned free viewer licenses which contradicts current policy.

Customer Quote(s)

* Customer: "everybody who wants to browse through recorded calls needs an AskElephant paid license at least the essential seat. Right?"
* Customer: "I also heard from other Relux colleague that you guys actually have a free viewer license, which then threw me off a little bit. That's why I was then also explicitly asking because it's wasn't consistent with what you were saying. So now it turns out that you guys don't have a viewer license."

Context / Details
The customer's previous tool (Mojo) offered free viewer licenses, which allowed their larger team (33 users: 12 recorders, 21 listeners) to access calls without additional cost. AskElephant's model requires a minimum of $25/month for essential seats.

Impact
The lack of clarity and the cost associated with viewer-only seats impact the perceived value and budget alignment for broader organizational adoption of AskElephant. This is a significant factor in their budget exceeding their target.

issues.description:
Is there a way we can move all the experimental internal-only feature flags to a separate workspace and/or make impersonation show exactly what that user should see? When handling support tickets I come across issues where I am playing blind because we enable feature flags that make our version of the site look/behave differently than what they see.

For example, today I was handing a ticket where the user wanted to merge companies together. Because on my AskElephant I see the new customers page I no longer see that button so I had to play off memory to remember where it was/what it was called. I have also had calls where I am walking users through how to use the app and realized that they are seeing something different than I am which causes confusion for them.

At the very least it would be nice to have all feature-flag related things tagged as such in-app so we know when talking to customers what is behind a feature flag and what is not.
issues.title: Separate experimental feature flags into dedicated workspace and improve impersonation to show user-specific views

issues.description:
Hi Team! I think i found that part of the reason why I find myself using ChatGPT instead of askelephant is for two reasons:

* On the AskElephant section I have too many chats, it would be awesome to organize those so if I use a chat to write emails I can keep all of those centralized
* When I copy a section from askelephant and paste it into an email or anywhere else it pastes the grey background as well whereas Chat doesnt do that

I would love to fully use AskElephant instead of stumbling to other sites
issues.title: Organize chats in AskElephant and remove grey background on paste

issues.description:
Here's an example of a time when it'd be useful to have 'versions' of workflows. This is an internal example where I made a change that the team wanted to revert because of how messy the channel got. We receive similar requests from clients often asking things like:

> "Can we go back to the setup we had last week? This new one isn't as good as the old one."

Ideally, I'd be able to see a version/save history where I could restore the old version of the workflow. Instead, I had to scroll through the workflow history to find the old prompt we were using, reformat it, and paste it back into the workflow.

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/c21987f2-7916-4051-a1c0-21bcb2834b1d/f77dcc1f-1dd5-4ec1-bc7f-13c616262584/dec375d1-600e-48b2-ae82-7118d3fd8b22)
issues.title: Add version history to restore previous workflow states

issues.title: Determine if Notetaker bot stops screenshare recording when pinning a speaker
